moon_phase.new.name: New Moon
moon_phase.new.themes: Quiet reset, clarifying motives, committing to internal alignment.
moon_phase.new.guidance: Slow down and reset. Listen to your body and choose one clear intention instead of many goals.
moon_phase.waxing_crescent.name: Waxing Crescent
moon_phase.waxing_crescent.themes: Take first micro-actions, share drafts, invite accountability.
moon_phase.waxing_crescent.guidance: Take small first steps, but don’t rush to show everything yet. Let ideas grow before sharing widely.
moon_phase.first_quarter.name: First Quarter
moon_phase.first_quarter.themes: Hit resistance that demands refinement or firmer commitment.
moon_phase.first_quarter.guidance: Notice what feels difficult or blocked. Decide whether to adjust your approach or commit more fully.
moon_phase.waxing_gibbous.name: Waxing Gibbous
moon_phase.waxing_gibbous.themes: Fine-tuning strategy, securing resources, aligning collaborators.
moon_phase.waxing_gibbous.guidance: Check details, expectations, and resources. Make sure everything is ready before presenting results.
moon_phase.full.name: Full Moon
moon_phase.full.themes: Insight arrives; truths are revealed; emotional tides peak.
moon_phase.full.guidance: Share what you’ve reached, acknowledge emotions, and release what no longer fits your path.
moon_phase.waning_gibbous.name: Waning Gibbous
moon_phase.waning_gibbous.themes: Harvest, integration, and teaching from what surfaced.
moon_phase.waning_gibbous.guidance: Reflect on what worked. Write down lessons and thank people who supported you.
moon_phase.last_quarter.name: Last Quarter
moon_phase.last_quarter.guidance: Let go of unnecessary tasks and commitments. Simplify plans and remove what drains energy.
moon_phase.waning_crescent.name: Waning Crescent
moon_phase.waning_crescent.themes: Restoration, dreams, and gestating the next cycle.
moon_phase.waning_crescent.guidance: Rest more, reduce input, and avoid major decisions. Pay attention to subtle physical and emotional signals.
weekly.waxing_crescent.A1.title: Start small
weekly.waxing_crescent.A1.summary: Momentum is building, but it’s early. Focus on one small move that proves you’re in motion—even if the plan isn’t perfect yet.
weekly.waxing_crescent.A1.cta: Say one sentence you want to be able to say by Friday: “This week I…”.
weekly.waxing_crescent.A2.title: Name what you feel
weekly.waxing_crescent.A2.summary: Feelings get heavier when they stay unspoken. If something is bothering you, name it early and simply—before it turns into pressure.
weekly.waxing_crescent.A2.cta: Record a 30-second voice note: “By Friday, I want to feel…”.
weekly.full.A6.title: Relationship reality check
weekly.full.A6.summary: This phase puts relationships in focus. Notice where you want more closeness—and where you need clearer boundaries.
weekly.full.A6.cta: Choose one conversation and say: “Here’s what matters to me.”
weekly.waning_gibbous.A4.title: Keep what worked
weekly.waning_gibbous.A4.summary: This is a good moment to reflect and keep the useful parts. Writing it down helps you repeat success instead of starting from zero next cycle.
weekly.waning_gibbous.A4.cta: Write three things you want to repeat next cycle.
weekly.waning_crescent.any.title: Slow down on purpose
weekly.waning_crescent.any.summary: Reduce input and lower the pressure. Rest more, move slower, and listen to what your body is asking for.
weekly.waning_crescent.any.cta: Pick one day with no big decisions—just observe.
check_in.emotional_pressure_release.prom: How are you holding emotions right now? Where does your body feel most dense?
check_in.emotional_pressure_release.step: Name the emotion or shade (for example, “static tension”).
Identify who this feeling is currently directed toward.
check_in.emotional_pressure_release.cta: Take three cycles of So Hum breathing to ground the sensation.
check_in.clarity_commitment.prompt: What do you want to feel after today?
check_in.clarity_commitment.steps.2: Notice whether your body resists this intention.
check_in.clarity_commitment.cta: Walk briskly for five minutes and repeat Om Ram three times on the exhale.
mantra.solar_radiance.title: Solar Radiance
mantra.solar_radiance.guidance: Place both palms on the solar plexus, breathe warmth, and chant on the exhale.
mantra.solar_radiance.notes: Use when the Meaning Block emphasizes visibility and ownership.
mantra.lunar_soften.title: Lunar Soften
mantra.lunar_soften.guidance: Silently say “So” on the inhale and “Hum” on the exhale while letting the shoulders drop.
mantra.lunar_soften.notes: Use in Context callouts when Lite highlights emotional protection.
ritual.grounding_salt_bath.title: Grounding Salt Bath
ritual.grounding_salt_bath.intention: Release emotional tension and return to the body
Add two handfuls of sea salt and a few drops of lavender.
Sit in silence, observe your breathing, and speak your intention.
ritual.grounding_salt_bath.notes: Recommend after a Meaning Block about processing or overthinking.
ritual.solar_walk_intent.title: Solar Walk Intent
ritual.solar_walk_intent.instructions.1: Walk briskly for five minutes while counting breaths.
ritual.solar_walk_intent.instructions.2: Pause to write one goal you are supporting today.
ritual.solar_walk_intent.instructions.3: Repeat “Om Ram” three times and feel warmth in the chest.
ritual.solar_walk_intent.notes: Pairs with Tarot Flow recommendations about action.

aspects.sun_saturn.summary: Sun and Saturn set how seriously you take yourself; this contact exposes the contract between ambition and self-respect.
aspects.sun_saturn.integration: Full context teaches how to balance accountability with warmth so structure fuels confidence instead of shutting it down.
aspects.sun_mars.summary: Sun with Mars describes how you pursue goals the moment a spark arrives; it drives assertion, heat, and risk tolerance.
aspects.sun_mars.integration: Full report channels that fire into repeatable actions that protect energy and relationships while keeping courage intact.
aspects.moon_saturn.summary: Moon–Saturn contacts reveal where caution protects your soft spots and why certain moods slow down expression.
aspects.moon_saturn.integration: Full report pairs boundaries with soothing practices so emotional steadiness does not harden into emotional distance.
aspects.moon_venus.summary: Moon and Venus describe how comfort, desire, and attachment blend; this is the emotional texture of intimacy.
aspects.moon_venus.integration: Full context shows how to honor tenderness while still naming needs so harmony does not erase honest feedback.
aspects.mercury_saturn.summary: Mercury–Saturn signatures shape mental pacing, skepticism, and how you trust your own conclusions.
aspects.mercury_saturn.integration: Full report offers scripts for translating rigorous thinking into collaborative language others can receive.
aspects.venus_mars.summary: Venus–Mars dynamics describe how attraction turns into action; it fuels magnetism, chemistry, and creative tension.
aspects.venus_mars.integration: Full context guides how to sync pursuit rhythm with intimacy so friction stays playful instead of exhausting.

numerology.life_path.title: Life Path
numerology.life_path.summary.1: A 1 life path is about independence and initiative—not isolation, but noticing where your own voice matters even when it diverges from expectations. It is the courage to name a direction that you feel inside before it has a tidy label.
numerology.life_path.summary.2: A 2 life path is about partnership and emotional attunement—not losing yourself in others, but learning which bonds actually nourish you versus familiar interaction patterns. Quality of contact matters more than the count of connections.
numerology.life_path.summary.3: A 3 life path is about expression and storytelling—not performing for approval, but noticing where your voice truly matters and where talk becomes a way to dodge depth. Some stories want to be told; some need to stay inside until they are ripe.
numerology.life_path.summary.4: A 4 life path is about structure and steady practice—not a cage of rules, but noticing which containers help you build and which habits only shrink you. Patience with form is how durable work gets finished.
numerology.life_path.summary.5: A 5 life path is about experiments and change—not endless novelty to avoid depth, but noticing where movement opens real options versus where it distracts from what already works. Curiosity is healthiest when it serves truth, not panic.
numerology.life_path.summary.6: A 6 life path is about care and relational harmony—not chronic self-sacrifice, but noticing where tending others is genuine and where it avoids your own needs. Balance between compassion for others and honesty with yourself is the lesson.
numerology.life_path.summary.7: A 7 life path is about analysis and intuition—not hiding from life in your head, but noticing when thinking protects you versus when it postpones action. Some questions need solitude; some need a single brave step.
numerology.life_path.summary.8: An 8 life path is about mastery and stewardship—not control for its own sake, but noticing where competence builds trust versus where it masks vulnerability. Power is cleanest when it is paired with integrity.
numerology.life_path.summary.9: A 9 life path is about empathy, closure, and mentoring—not rescuing everyone to avoid your own growth, but noticing where support is truly needed versus where people are simply used to you carrying the weight. Completion includes letting others walk their path.

compat.modality.cardinal_fixed: balance between starting and holding steady
compat.modality.cardinal_mutable: fast adaptation with a rush risk
compat.modality.fixed_mutable: a gap in flexibility versus inertia
compat.context.just_met: just getting to know each other
compat.sign.ctx.just_met: You're at the start: lots of guesses and fast reads — below is how this pair usually unfolds.
compat.sign.ctx.mutual_attraction: There's clear attraction — next it depends on how you negotiate pace and honesty.
compat.sign.ctx.in_relationship: You're already in contact — below is about repeating patterns, not the first spark.
compat.sign.ctx.unclear: The situation is fuzzy — naming the dynamic helps you stop guessing and bending facts.
compat.sign.ctx.conflict_distance: There's distance or conflict — below is the typical loop and where it can break.
compat.sign.ctx.split_but_pull: You're not formally together but it pulls — below is how attraction depends on clarity and boundaries.
compat.sign.ctx.unspecified: Below isn't a sign cheat sheet — it's the usual contact dynamic between you.
compat.sign.overview.bridge: Between {from_name} and {to_name} you’ll notice a «{element_relation}» thread. Early on that often feels like recognition or interest — but it doesn’t erase pace differences.
compat.sign.overview.rhythm: Different pacing shows up wherever «{rhythm_relation}» kicks in. One of you may move into contact faster while the other steps back to stay in control or not burn out.
compat.sign.overview.not_verdict: This isn't a verdict on compatibility — it's dynamics that are easier to hold once they're named.
compat.sign.overview.extra.conflict: Right now it matters more to see the repeating fight/silence script than «who's right on the topic».
compat.sign.overview.extra.split: When it pulls after a breakup, separate attraction from a real shot at contact without the old loop.
compat.tagline.t2: strong pull and sharp conflict edges
compat.tagline.t3: steadier day-to-day than spark and novelty
compat.tagline.t4: strong chemistry — don't confuse it with safety
compat.tagline.t5: the bond shifts by phase — normal if you talk about it
compat.tagline.t6: strong spark — the question is who sets pace and boundaries
compat.tagline.t7: you pull toward each other — watch it doesn't become a chase for attention
compat.tagline.t8: chemistry is clear — daily life shows if you hold without drama
compat.tagline.t9: repair after ruptures comes easier — don't treat that as guaranteed
compat.tagline.t10: the dynamic is alive — don't confuse a phase with a verdict
compat.static.para1: {from_name} and {to_name} align fastest where neither tries to force the other's pace.
compat.static.para2: The pair's strength is {element_relation}. That usually shows up in first contact, chemistry, or how easily you return to dialogue.
compat.static.para3: The main friction zone is {rhythm_relation}. The clearer you name expectations and decision speed, the less unnecessary tension.
compat.static.para4: This bond holds best on simple agreements: what matters, where initiative lives, and how you discuss hard things without guessing.
compat.static.para5: To go deeper, the next level is less about signs and more about real dates, profile, and lived contact patterns.
compat.quick.headline.hi: Between you, connection shows up fast and is easier to sustain than for many pairs.
compat.quick.headline.mid: Solid potential — this pair opens up better through tuning than on autopilot.
compat.quick.headline.low: It works if you don't leave contact to chance and agree on what matters early.
compat.quick.headline.min: This pair needs more care, clarity, and patience than instant matching.
compat.quick.strongest.hi: What works best here is {element_relation}: it's easier to catch a shared tone and return to contact.
compat.quick.strongest.mid: The pair's strength is {element_relation}. You can build trust on that if you don't lose clarity in how you talk.
compat.quick.strongest.low: The anchor is {element_relation}. That's the foothold for contact without extra pressure.
compat.quick.strongest.min: Even in a tough mix there's still an anchor — {element_relation}. Lean on that, not on instant matching.
compat.quick.friction.hi: Friction usually starts not in feelings but where «{rhythm_relation}» shows up and you stop checking pace with each other.
compat.quick.friction.mid: The main risk is {rhythm_relation}. That's where resentment stacks from mismatched expectations.
compat.quick.friction.low: The weak spot is {rhythm_relation}. Without spoken rules it turns into push-pull fast.
compat.quick.friction.min: Main tension comes from {rhythm_relation}. If you ignore it, the bond tips into stubbornness or withdrawal.
compat.quick.next.hi: Don't rely only on ease. Agree early how you hold contact on ordinary daily topics.
compat.quick.next.mid: Next step: clarify expectations and pace — who ramps in faster, who needs more time, and how you talk about tension.
compat.quick.next.low: Name boundaries and how you discuss hard topics. That makes this pairing steadier.
compat.quick.next.min: Don't force closeness. First understand how you each enter contact and how not to hurt each other with that gap.
compat.quick.strength_extra: Direct talk without extra guessing helps this bond.
compat.quick.caution_extra: Don't wait for alignment to appear by itself if pace and expectations stay unnamed.
compat.dynamics.tendency.cardinal: more likely to initiate contact and steer turning points
compat.dynamics.tendency.fixed: more likely to hold pace and boundaries, slower to change
compat.dynamics.tendency.mutable: more likely to adapt and redirect the conversation
compat.dynamics.tendency.default: enters contact differently in different phases
compat.dynamics.emotional.diff: different depth and speed of closing off emotionally
compat.dynamics.emotional.same: similar emotional tempo, different ways of protecting
compat.role.you.cardinal.1: you move into contact faster and set the pace of getting closer
compat.role.you.cardinal.2: you want clarity and an answer sooner
compat.role.you.fixed.1: you hold your line and dislike being yanked around
compat.role.you.fixed.2: you cling to stability more once you're already in contact
compat.role.you.mutable.1: you adapt flexibly but may drift into what's unsaid
compat.role.you.mutable.2: you often seek compromise instead of head-on collision
compat.role.you.default.1: you enter contact differently depending on the phase
compat.role.partner.cardinal.1: often leads the conversation and initiates turns
compat.role.partner.cardinal.2: may read a pause as losing interest
compat.role.partner.fixed.1: holds distance and controls the pace of closeness
compat.role.partner.fixed.2: opens slower than you sometimes want
compat.role.partner.mutable.1: smooths and redirects when it heats up
compat.role.partner.mutable.2: may retreat into generalities instead of a clear yes/no
compat.role.partner.default.1: enters contact their own way — not always matching your expectation
compat.sexintensity.deep: very high — feelings and physicality run deep
compat.sexintensity.strong: strong — closeness and proof of importance through touch matter
compat.sexintensity.fast: strong — you're pulled together sexually fast
compat.sexintensity.good: solid — attraction exists if daily tension doesn't smother it
compat.sexintensity.low: not the main engine — it needs deliberate upkeep
compat.block.emotions.title: Emotional fit
compat.block.emotions.subtitle: Who goes deeper, who shuts down, who pulls for closeness
compat.block.emotions.take.diff: One of you feels emotions longer and deeper; the other moves toward rationality or distance faster.
compat.block.emotions.take.same: You read each other's emotional backdrop faster — the risk is both stewing, then flipping into «hard mode» together.
compat.block.emotions.detail.diff: Against «{element_relation}» one may feel a missing response; the other may feel pressure or emotional control.
compat.block.emotions.detail.same: Similar element helps read mood, but fights often aren't about facts — they're about «how important this is right now».
compat.block.emotions.risk: If needs aren't named sooner, the slower side starts «reading silence» and misreads.
compat.block.emotions.action: Agree one signal for «I need a pause» and how you return to the conversation.
compat.block.communication.title: Communication
compat.block.communication.subtitle: Directness, avoidance, what's left unsaid
compat.block.communication.take.diff: You don't always match on style: one wants straight words; the other wants time and soft framing.
compat.block.communication.take.same: You may speak «the same language» but dodge uncomfortable topics until it's late.
compat.block.communication.detail: The rhythm backdrop is «{rhythm_relation}» — talks cut off mid-sentence or jump topics without decisions.
compat.block.communication.risk: What's unsaid turns into tests, hints, and «you should have known» resentment.
compat.block.communication.action: One concrete question instead of a monologue — and time to answer without interrogation.
compat.block.conflicts.title: Conflict
compat.block.conflicts.subtitle: Who escalates, who shuts down, how to close the loop
compat.block.conflicts.take: Conflict often runs on reaction, not topic: one ramps tension; the other withdraws or closes.
compat.block.conflicts.detail: A typical loop is pressure → distance → more pressure, especially with «{rhythm_relation}» in the pair.
compat.block.conflicts.action: A stop phrase for raised voices and a 15-minute slot «without armor» to unpack.
compat.block.sexuality.title: Sexual dynamic
compat.block.sexuality.subtitle: Sex, desire, initiative, tension
compat.block.sexuality.detail.mid: {initiator} {control_line} That can make sex intense yet unpredictable on safety — especially if one needs proof of worth through the body and the other needs control over pace.
compat.block.sexuality.risk: Sex doesn't erase what's unsaid at home — it muffles it, then it returns louder.
compat.block.sexuality.action: Talk separately about what each of you wants in sex, what pace feels right, and where pressure becomes a red line.
compat.block.sexuality.initiator.diff: Whoever moves faster into contact often takes the first step toward sex — that is not the same as being ready for vulnerability after.
compat.block.sexuality.initiator.same: Initiative can be shared — both wait for a signal and both fear seeming too eager about desire.
compat.block.sexuality.control.fixed: Control often hides as «the right pace» or coolness — especially if one sign holds the boundary harder.
compat.block.sexuality.control.other: Control can show up as minimizing or postponing the important talk.
compat.block.sexuality.tip.initiative_al: Take turns leading: face-to-face with eye contact if you're both fast; slow pace with long pauses if one of you needs more warmup.
compat.block.sexuality.tip.initiative_sh: If you both wait for a signal — decide who asks today: one line like «I want you now» or «let's go slow» breaks the stalemate.
compat.block.sexuality.tip.position_pace: For your pair, positions where you see each other's reactions and can change pace without jerking often work — side-lying or on top with weight on hands, not full body pressure.
compat.block.sexuality.tip.high_drive: With strong pull, agree on a stop word and that after intense sex you take a short pause for water and words — otherwise the body cools faster than the emotions.
compat.block.sexuality.tip.warmup: If spark isn't instant — start with 10–15 minutes of touch without a finish goal: contact, breath, a direct «what feels good right now».
compat.block.sexuality.tip.if_rejected: If your partner says no — don't test again the same night; ask «when would it feel okay to revisit this» and shift to non-sexual closeness without sulking.
compat.block.sexuality.tip.fixed_pace: If one of you needs steady rhythm — pick one position longer and change only pace and depth, not the whole scene every two minutes.
compat.block.long_term.title: Long-term
compat.block.long_term.subtitle: Foundation, goals, fatigue over time
compat.block.long_term.take.low: Over time the bond can ride on pull and shared meaning — but without agreements it turns tiring.
compat.block.long_term.take.high: There's a base for the long haul if you don't swap patience for avoiding hard topics.
compat.block.long_term.detail: Right now the foothold is «{strongest}». Long-run risk is enduring what should be spoken before resentment stacks.
compat.block.long_term.risk: If you only match on intensity, not agreements — the bond burns out.
compat.block.long_term.action: One shared 3-month horizon: not a slogan — practical and emotional ground rules.
compat.scenario.closer.title: If you want to get closer
compat.scenario.closer.b1: Don't push pace — offer a small step and a specific time.
compat.scenario.closer.b2: Leave room without a «who wins the silence» game.
compat.scenario.closer.b3: Once say out loud what closeness means to you — without blame.
compat.scenario.clarity.title: If you want clarity
compat.scenario.clarity.b1: Ask one direct question and don't stretch ambiguity into «later».
compat.scenario.clarity.b2: Write agreements in plain language: «we do this / we don't do this».
compat.scenario.clarity.b3: Don't mix clarity with ultimatum — tone makes the difference.
compat.scenario.exit.title: If you want to leave or lower involvement
compat.scenario.exit.b1: Watch actions and patterns, not chat explanations.
compat.scenario.exit.b2: Don't keep contact to prove you're «good enough».
compat.scenario.exit.b3: If it pulls after a breakup — list three concrete pain repeats and check what changed.
compat.scenario.insert.just_met: At the start curiosity beats fast «forever» conclusions.
compat.scenario.insert.conflict: Lower escalation first — then the meaning of the fight.
guidance.page.heroLead: Everything on one page: scroll top to bottom—spread, question, optional context, then cards and your reading request.
guidance.page.compatHint: For couples there’s also Compatibility—this reading flow still handles relationship dynamics well.
guidance.page.unauthLead: Sign in to lay a spread on one screen and get a personal answer that respects your profile.
guidance.page.sectionSpreadLead: Short or expanded—match the situation. Changing the format clears any cards you already dealt.
guidance.page.clarifyOneCardLockedNote: Right now only the clarifying “one card” spread is available—the main spread stays locked until you finish or choose “{startOver}”.
guidance.page.sectionQuestionLead: Ask for clarity and action—not a guaranteed outcome. The more concrete the wording, the more useful the answer.
guidance.page.questionPlaceholder:
Examples:
• What's important for me to understand here?
• What's the wisest move for me right now?
• What am I missing in my own reaction?
guidance.page.questionLockedMain: The main question is locked for this reading: “{question}”
guidance.page.questionAvoidHint: Avoid “will they definitely…” and “will I definitely get…”—this isn’t about predicting outcomes.
guidance.page.sectionContextLead: Topic and intent help tailor the reading. You can leave everything unset.
guidance.page.fieldRelationshipRole: Who this person is to you
guidance.page.clarifyGoalLead: Pick one narrow focus—so the reading doesn't turn into endless back-and-forth.
guidance.page.cardsLeadClarify: One card in the “Focus” position. Deal and reveal it—then request clarification (the server allows at most one per main reading).
guidance.page.cardsLeadNormal: Shuffle for “{spreadTitle}”, then reveal each position one at a time. When everything is open and your question is ready—request the reading.
guidance.page.dealReadyHint: Tap “Deal cards” when you're ready to focus on your question.
guidance.page.cardsRevealHint: Reveal every position—then the “Get reading” button becomes active.
guidance.page.submitHintClarify: Pick a clarification goal above and reveal the card.
guidance.page.submitHintQuestion: Refine your question above first (at least three characters).
guidance.page.loadingLeadClarify: We're linking one card to your main reading and the goal you chose.
guidance.page.loadingLeadMain: We weigh your question, positions, and profile—it's a coherent take for your situation, not a dictionary of meanings.

guidance.page.toast.clarifyOneCardOnly: Clarification is only for the “one card” spread with one open position
guidance.page.toast.clarifyAlready409: You already used a clarification for this reading—start a new session with a spread.
guidance.page.toast.readingFailed: Could not assemble the reading. Check your network and try again.
guidance.page.toast.feedbackSavedUnclear: Noted that you need more clarity
guidance.page.toast.clarifyAlreadyUsed: Clarification for this reading was already used
guidance.result.reflectionFallback: What matters more for you right now: easing the tension quickly, or getting clarity you can test with one action?
guidance.result.toast.feedbackThanks: Thanks—that helps us improve future readings
guidance.result.assessmentEyebrow: Your question and the spread’s strength
guidance.result.safetyBanner: This reading can help you see emotions and inner conflict, but it isn’t a substitute for professional support or checking facts on the ground. If safety, money, health, or paperwork is involved—rely on real information and qualified help.
guidance.result.coreEyebrow: The main knot in the situation
guidance.result.continueEyebrow: If you want to go further
guidance.result.compatLead: If there’s a specific person, you can go deeper on the dynamic—emotions, conflict, closeness, and perspective.
guidance.result.patternLead: If this keeps showing up, your portrait is a good place to see the script.
guidance.result.clarifyLead: One card with a specific aim. The server allows at most one clarification per main reading.
guidance.result.commentLabel: What should we remember for your next readings?
guidance.result.profileIncompleteHint: This answer was assembled without a full profile—fill in your portrait for more precise readings.

compat.funnel.llm_base.title: Deep layer (Base Model)
compat.funnel.score_semantics: Percentages reflect how easy it is for this area to work without conscious effort — not fate or a guarantee. High means easier; low means you need rules and clarity.
compat.funnel.confidence_note.signs_only: Low confidence: little personal data — treat scores as directional, not deterministic.
compat.funnel.confidence_note.birth_date: Medium confidence: dates help, but without time/place and psychological context some areas remain inferred.
compat.funnel.confidence_note.full_profi: Higher confidence: time and place are in the chart — scores are more grounded, but psychology and context still matter.
compat.funnel.domain.love.label: Love & pull
compat.funnel.domain.sex.label: Sex & sexuality
compat.funnel.domain.emotional.label: Emotional steadiness
compat.funnel.domain.conflicts.label: Conflict & repair
compat.funnel.domain.money.label: Money & risk
compat.funnel.domain.family.label: Family & home life
compat.funnel.domain.work.label: Work & shared logistics
compat.funnel.domain.children.label: Children & parenting
compat.funnel.driver.neutral_raise: There are levers you can strengthen with clear agreements.
compat.funnel.driver.neutral_lower: Some areas heat up fast without explicit conversation rules.
compat.funnel.driver.attr_low: Pull is uneven or quickly turns into testing
compat.funnel.driver.stab_low: Rhythm breaks easily: fatigue, withdrawal, unfinished talks
compat.funnel.driver.repair_high: After ruptures it is easier to return without total trust wipeout
compat.funnel.driver.repair_low: After tension repair is hard — silence, resentment, or pressure loops
compat.funnel.driver.sex_low: Physical intimacy needs explicit honesty and pacing
compat.funnel.driver.element_diff: Different elements add freshness but different need languages
compat.funnel.driver.fire_water: Fire–water contrast amplifies swings unless you name them aloud
compat.funnel.driver.modality_diff: Different operating tempo: initiation, holding, and flexibility do not match
compat.funnel.driver.modality_same: Similar tempo makes everyday coordination easier
compat.funnel.driver.money_grounded: Earth/water mix — easier to agree on spending and reserves
compat.funnel.driver.family_water: Emotional care and support — a plus for home and family
compat.funnel.control.lead_you: You more often set the pace — your partner may read it as control or support.
compat.funnel.control.lead_partner: Your partner more often sets the pace — keep your voice on boundaries.
compat.funnel.control.shared: Leadership is not fixed; it can swap by topic.
compat.funnel.clarity.you_withdraw: You tend to pause or protect yourself sooner when pressed or unheard.
compat.funnel.clarity.partner_withdraw: Your partner tends to pause under pressure — directness only works with tone and timing.
compat.funnel.clarity.balanced: Withdrawal is more shared — agree how you return to the conversation.
compat.funnel.clarity.ctx.unspecified: Context unspecified — treat this as a frame, not a final verdict.
compat.funnel.clarity.ctx.just_met: Early on, do not confuse chemistry with promises — watch actions.
compat.funnel.clarity.ctx.mutual_attract: With clear attraction, honesty about pace and boundaries matters more than «who likes more».
compat.funnel.clarity.ctx.in_relationshi: In an ongoing bond, recurring cycles beat one-off explanations.
compat.funnel.clarity.ctx.unclear: In ambiguity, one clear request beats endless interpretation.
compat.funnel.clarity.ctx.conflict_dista: In conflict, lower escalation first — then meaning — or you train resentment.
compat.funnel.clarity.ctx.split_but_pull: After a breakup, separate attraction from actual safety to reconnect.
compat.funnel.timeline.now.title: Now
compat.funnel.timeline.now.body: Active phase: {context_label}. Watch what repeats 2–3 times, not the ideal story.
compat.funnel.timeline.near.title: Near term
compat.funnel.timeline.near.body: If conversation rules do not change, the strongest trigger (pace, control, or silence) intensifies.
compat.funnel.timeline.inertia.title: If nothing changes
compat.funnel.timeline.inertia.body: Inertia deepens the same loop at higher emotional cost — «enduring it» becomes normal.
compat.funnel.timeline.conscious.title: If you act consciously
compat.funnel.timeline.conscious.body: One clear weekly agreement plus direct tone without ultimatums usually shifts dynamics faster than a monthly «big talk».
compat.funnel.risk.ok.headline: Green band
compat.funnel.risk.ok.when_ok: When you keep tone in check and pause without disappearing, contact stabilizes.
compat.funnel.risk.ok.when_shifts: When «who can stay silent longer» wins or topics turn into character attacks.
compat.funnel.risk.ok.when_breaks: When ultimatums, humiliation, or message control become habitual tools.
compat.funnel.risk.caution.headline: Yellow band
compat.funnel.risk.caution.when_ok: When you anchor facts and ask for specifics instead of guessing.
compat.funnel.risk.caution.when_shifts: When «we will talk later» becomes endless deferral and stacking.
compat.funnel.risk.caution.when_breaks: When any fight ends with total respect wipeout.
compat.funnel.risk.critical.headline: Red band
compat.funnel.risk.critical.when_ok: Only with external support (therapy/mediation) and firm safety boundaries.
compat.funnel.risk.critical.when_shifts: When fear, jealousy, or control becomes the main glue.
compat.funnel.risk.critical.when_breaks: When threats, coercion, or systematic humiliation appear — this is safety, not «compatibility».
compat.funnel.basis.love.h: Strong pull plus emotional padding — easier to build closeness with everyday honesty.
compat.funnel.basis.love.m: Attraction exists but needs pacing and expectation talks.
compat.funnel.basis.love.l: The love layer needs work — hope without clear contact rules hurts.
compat.funnel.basis.sex.h: Body response and initiative align well — do not let it mask unspoken home tension.
compat.funnel.basis.sex.m: Chemistry varies — hold it with stated boundaries and consent without testing.
compat.funnel.basis.sex.l: Sexual layer is sensitive to tension and control — safety and tone before acceleration.
compat.funnel.basis.emotional.h: Emotions are easier to steady — fewer swings from rhythm mismatch.
compat.funnel.basis.emotional.m: Waves are normal — you need a clear return-to-talk path.
compat.funnel.basis.emotional.l: Emotional steadiness is strained — higher risk of shutdowns and automatic resentment.
compat.funnel.basis.conflicts.h: After ruptures repair is easier — less loop of «winning the fight».
compat.funnel.basis.conflicts.m: Conflict needs tone/time rules or the same circle repeats.
compat.funnel.basis.conflicts.l: Conflict cycle is rigid — without external structure, distance or pressure takes over.
compat.funnel.basis.money.h: Money talks are easier — less hidden competition or «spend tests».
compat.funnel.basis.money.m: Different risk appetite is fine if roles and transparency exist.
compat.funnel.basis.money.l: Money is provocative — high risk of control, shame, or devaluation.
compat.funnel.basis.family.h: Home and family themes land softer — more patience and shared rituals.
compat.funnel.basis.family.m: Domestic friction will come — do not turn it into «who lives right».
compat.funnel.basis.family.l: Family layer is heavy without clear duty boundaries and post-fight repair.
compat.funnel.basis.work_live.h: Easier to sync tasks and responsibility — fewer mutual sabotages.
compat.funnel.basis.work_live.m: Ambition and rhythm differ — you need explicit roles and check-ins.
compat.funnel.basis.work_live.l: Work/home logistics clash — easy to devalue each other's contribution.
compat.funnel.basis.children.h: If relevant, parenting reads calmer — more alignment on boundaries and patience.
compat.funnel.basis.children.m: Different care styles are normal — agree a minimum rule set and support.
compat.funnel.basis.children.l: High overload and control fights — hard to avoid getting stuck without mediation.
compat.funnel.improve.love.h: Pick one weekly honesty practice without interrogation — one concrete request, not a tribunal.
compat.funnel.improve.love.m: Align definitions of closeness and ban one fight move (sarcasm, vanishing, ultimatums).
compat.funnel.improve.love.l: Start with a micro-agreement on response pace and a 24h pause before heavy talks.
compat.funnel.improve.sex.h: Monthly 10-minute talk on desire/boundaries without blame.
compat.funnel.improve.sex.m: State what yes/no/later means for each — no mind-reading.
compat.funnel.improve.sex.l: Lower general home/conflict tension first — otherwise sex becomes a control hostage.
compat.funnel.improve.emotional.h: Reinforce repair: thank the small step toward each other.
compat.funnel.improve.emotional.m: Use a signal «I need 20 minutes» instead of disappearing.
compat.funnel.improve.emotional.l: One topic per talk — stop stacking issues.
compat.funnel.improve.conflicts.h: After a rupture — 10 minutes on facts, no character verdicts.
compat.funnel.improve.conflicts.m: Rule: lower tone first, then meaning.
compat.funnel.improve.conflicts.l: Consider external structure (couple/family therapist) before new commitments.
compat.funnel.improve.money.h: Lock a simple scheme: joint/personal spends and a no-debate cap.
compat.funnel.improve.money.m: Split roles: who tracks, who owns the reserve.
compat.funnel.improve.money.l: Stop money as a love test — move to transparent rules.
compat.funnel.improve.family.h: One weekly home ritual without phones.
compat.funnel.improve.family.m: Two-week duty list + permission to say «I am overloaded» without defensiveness.
compat.funnel.improve.family.l: Minimum: how each recovers after a fight — otherwise home becomes a battlefield.
compat.funnel.improve.work_live.l: Stop «who is more tired» — replace with redistribution.
compat.funnel.improve.children.h: Shared boundary rules + backup plan when one is depleted.
compat.funnel.improve.children.m: Weekly 15 minutes: what you appreciate in each other's care style.
compat.funnel.improve.children.l: Do not solve kid conflicts hot — move to a scheduled time.
guidance.catalog.spread.one_card.title: One card
guidance.catalog.spread.one_card.descrip: When you want a short answer, a focal point, or a nudge for the situation.
guidance.catalog.spread.one_card.fit0: what matters for me to understand?
guidance.catalog.spread.one_card.fit1: what should I pay attention to?
Not just “yes” or “no”, but a reading: what supports the situation, what gets in the way, and where the nuance is.
guidance.catalog.spread.guidance_what_ha: When the situation feels fuzzy, emotions are high, and there's no clear picture.
guidance.catalog.spread.three_cards.desc: A classic spread for a timeline: past, present, possible movement.
guidance.catalog.spread.guidance_choice_: When you need to decide between two paths.
guidance.catalog.spread.guidance_relatio: For questions about a person, feelings, distance, pull, conflict, or uncertainty.
guidance.catalog.spread.guidance_sexual_: When the question touches desire, attraction, closeness, distance, tension, or how your body responds.
guidance.catalog.spread.guidance_inner_c: When one part of you wants one thing and another part wants something else.
guidance.catalog.spread.guidance_work_mo: For calls about work, projects, money, career, negotiation, and risk.
guidance.catalog.spread.guidance_deep_ei: When the situation is layered and one answer won't cover it.

natal.map.hero.title: My natal chart
natal.map.hero.lead: The foundation for interpretations: positions, houses, and aspects. Portrait and “who you are” live in Profile; Today is for the day—without duplicating it here.
natal.map.hero.metric.lifePathHint: Numerology line used across personal layers.
natal.map.hero.metric.sunHint: Core expression style and the chart’s lead sign.
natal.map.hero.metric.ascHintSet: How you show up, connect, and make a first impression.
natal.map.hero.metric.ascHintMissing: Exact birth time and place are needed for a reliable Ascendant.
natal.map.hero.metric.mcHintSet: Career direction, responsibility, and public vector.
natal.map.hero.metric.mcHintMissing: MC depends on chart angles and may be missing without an exact birth time.
natal.map.hero.metric.archetypeHintFallb: Will fold into future personalized reads.
natal.map.next.title: What’s next?
natal.map.next.lead: Profile (portrait and life areas), Guidance on a theme, or Flow for rhythm. This screen isn’t about “today”.
natal.map.journey.s1.title: Read the chart’s main story
natal.map.journey.s1.body: First, notice your main supports, tensions, and recurring themes. Don’t try to read every house and aspect at once.
natal.map.journey.s2.title: Connect the chart to a question
natal.map.journey.s2.body: If you want situational guidance—not a positions encyclopedia—continue in Guidance. The daily layer stays on Today.
natal.map.journey.s3.title: Anchor it in real action
natal.map.journey.s3.bodyEditorial: Once the chart theme is clear, move it into the right next module instead of stopping at understanding alone.
natal.map.journey.s3.bodyGeneric: Once the chart theme is clear, translate it into practice, a question, compatibility, or calendar rhythm.
natal.map.wheel.title: Chart wheel
natal.map.wheel.subtitle: A visual map of planets, houses, and aspects—the geometry your interpretations build on.
natal.map.lifeAreas.title: The four angles
natal.map.lifeAreas.subtitle: A cut across houses 1, 4, 7, and 10—structural anchors. Thematic life areas (love, money, intimacy, body, etc.) are composed in Profile from several chart factors, not only these four cells.
natal.map.planets.title: Personal planets
natal.map.planets.subtitle: Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn—the most useful layer for character and patterns.
natal.map.planet.noRow: Planet data didn’t arrive. Check birth date, time, and place, then refresh the chart.
natal.map.allHouses.title: All houses
natal.map.allHouses.subtitle: Full map of life areas. Kept expandable so the main view stays light.
natal.map.aspects.title: Aspects between planets
natal.map.aspects.subtitle: Key links that show inner harmony, tension, and how you react.
natal.map.aspects.geometryNote: Showing basic aspects from geometry. Refresh the chart later for a fuller pass.
natal.map.aspects.empty.beforeLink: We need ecliptic longitudes (or sign + degree in sign) to compute aspects. Usually fixed by recalculating the chart:
natal.map.aspects.empty.afterLink: and reload this page. If the chart cache is stale, saving the profile will refresh it.
natal.map.angles.title: My baseline
natal.map.angles.subtitle: Ascendant and MC—how you’re read on first contact and how your visible path of achievement forms.
natal.map.angles.ascWithSign: Ascendant in {sign} describes your first impulse, how you connect with people, and how you enter new situations.
natal.map.angles.ascFallback: Ascendant isn’t defined precisely enough yet—usually the chart needs an exact birth time or reliable angles.
natal.map.angles.mcWithSign: MC in {sign} shows how you realize yourself in career, responsibility, and public role.
natal.map.angles.mcFallback: MC isn’t defined precisely enough yet—often tied to missing birth time or incomplete angles.
natal.map.editorial.subtitle: A tight read of chart structure. Long portrait and life areas stay in Profile—no daily copy here.
natal.map.editorial.summaryFallback: A fuller chart essence will appear after the full interpretation.
natal.map.editorial.giftsEmpty: Chart strengths will sharpen as personal reading accumulates.
natal.map.editorial.tensionsEmpty: Growth edges get clearer after houses and aspects are fully read.
natal.map.prompt.title: What the system uses in prompts
natal.map.prompt.subtitle: Short and transparent: the same chart elements are blended into Today, Guidance, and Compatibility—not the whole screen, only relevant nodes.
natal.map.prompt.body: These signals help keep tone coherent: Moon—emotional rhythm and sensitivity; Venus—love style and harmony; Mars—action and desire; 7th house—partnership and agreements; 8th house—intimacy, trust, shared resources; 6th house—body and daily rhythm; MC and 10th house—achievement and status. The profile selector picks a slice for the day’s task or question.
natal.map.bridge.title: Portrait and strengths
natal.map.bridge.subtitle: “Who you are”, growth edges, and life areas live in Profile—here we keep the chart as the foundation.
natal.map.bridge.leadBefore: To avoid repeating the same copy in Today and Guidance, see fuller character reads in
natal.map.profileLens.label: How this reads in the chart
natal.map.houseFallback.1: Identity, self-presentation, and how you enter life.
natal.map.houseFallback.2: Resources, money, values, and inner stability.
natal.map.houseFallback.3: Communication, learning, environment, and everyday exchange.
natal.map.houseFallback.4: Home, family, roots, and what makes you feel safe inside.
natal.map.houseFallback.5: Creativity, love, joy, children, and personal shine.
natal.map.houseFallback.6: Routine, work, health, and everyday discipline.
natal.map.houseFallback.7: Partnership, agreements, and relating to others.
natal.map.houseFallback.8: Depth, trust, transformation, and shared resources.
natal.map.houseFallback.9: Meaning, worldview, expansion, and inner faith.
natal.map.houseFallback.10: Career, status, direction of achievement, and recognition.
natal.map.houseFallback.11: Friends, community, big goals, and the horizon ahead.
natal.map.houseFallback.12: The unconscious, rest, recovery, and inner work.
natal.map.aspect.geometryFallbackDescrip: Ecliptic separation ~{sep}° (target {aspect} ~{angle}°). This is a geometric cue from longitudes; fuller wording comes from the server aspect layer when orbs match.
natal.map.softTrim.fallback: Description will be refined after a full read.
